ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) ProxyLogon chain — w3wp.exe spawning shells + ASPX web-shell drop in Exchange virtual directories
ucs.description: Hunts the signature post-exploitation chain Silk Typhoon (Microsoft = HAFNIUM, MSTIC cluster G0125, 'Operation Exchange Marauder' 2021 and 2024-2025 IT-supply-chain campaigns): on-prem Exchange / OWA / ECP exploitation where the IIS worker (w3wp.exe) hosting an Exchange app pool spawns cmd/powershell/discovery LOLBins and drops .aspx/.ashx web shells (China Chopper, OwaAuth, ChinaCopper variants) into Exchange front-end paths. The combined parent=w3wp + Exchange-AppPool + OWA/ECP path web-shell write is HAFNIUM's calling card and rarely produced by legitimate code paths.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.parent_process="*MSExchange*" OR Processes.parent_process="*OwaAppPool*" OR Processes.parent_process="*EcpAppPool*" OR Processes.parent_process="*RpcProxyAppPool*" OR Processes.parent_process="*ExchangeBackEnd*") Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","wmic.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","schtasks.exe","reg.exe","rundll32.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.process Processes.process_name Processes.dest _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=left host [| tstats summariesonly=false count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" (Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ClientAccess\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\V15\\FrontEnd\\*") (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asmx") by host Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.action _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename _time as file_write_time, file_path as webshell_path, file_name as webshell_file] | eval delta=abs(_time - file_write_time) | where isnull(delta) OR delta < 3600 | table _time host user parent_process process process_name webshell_path webshell_file delta | sort - _time
ucs.kql:
let exchange_paths = dynamic([@"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\", @"\ClientAccess\Owa\Auth\", @"\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\", @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\"]);
let WebshellWrites = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asmx"
    | where FolderPath has_any (exchange_paths) or FolderPath has @"\inetpub\wwwroot\"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | project FileWriteTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, WebshellFile = FileName, WebshellPath = FolderPath, WebshellSHA256 = SHA256;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MSExchange","OwaAppPool","EcpAppPool","RpcProxyAppPool","ExchangeBackEndAppPool","ECPAppPool")
| where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","wmic.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","schtasks.exe","reg.exe","rundll32.exe","hh.exe")
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| join kind=leftouter WebshellWrites on DeviceId
| where isnull(FileWriteTime) or abs(datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, FileWriteTime)) <= 60
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildBin = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          WebshellFile, WebshellPath, WebshellSHA256, FileWriteTime
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let exchange_paths = dynamic([@"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\", @"\ClientAccess\Owa\Auth\", @"\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\"]);
let WebshellWrites = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".aspx" or TargetFileName endswith ".ashx" or TargetFileName endswith ".asmx"
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (exchange_paths) or TargetFilePath has @"\inetpub\wwwroot\"
    | where ActingProcessName endswith "w3wp.exe"
    | project FileWriteTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, WebshellFile = TargetFileName, WebshellPath = TargetFilePath;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "w3wp.exe"
| where NewProcessName has_any ("\\cmd.exe","\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe","\\net.exe","\\net1.exe","\\whoami.exe","\\systeminfo.exe","\\ipconfig.exe","\\nltest.exe","\\tasklist.exe","\\wmic.exe","\\certutil.exe","\\bitsadmin.exe","\\schtasks.exe","\\reg.exe","\\rundll32.exe")
| where Account !endswith "$"
| join kind=leftouter WebshellWrites on $left.Computer == $right.DvcHostname
| where isempty(tostring(FileWriteTime)) or abs(datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, FileWriteTime)) <= 60
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, WebshellFile, WebshellPath, FileWriteTime
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) Exchange w3wp.exe Spawns Shell or Discovery LOLBin
id: 6f1b8d52-9c4f-4a26-8f24-7d4a23c4e3a1
status: experimental
description: Detects HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon ProxyLogon-style post-exploitation where the IIS worker hosting an Exchange app pool (OwaAppPool, EcpAppPool, RpcProxyAppPool, etc.) spawns a command interpreter or discovery LOLBin. This is the most signature TTP of cluster G0125 since 2021 and recurring 2024-2025 IT-supply-chain operations.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  parent_iis:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  parent_exchange_pool:
    ParentCommandLine|contains:
      - 'MSExchange'
      - 'OwaAppPool'
      - 'EcpAppPool'
      - 'RpcProxyAppPool'
      - 'ExchangeBackEndAppPool'
  child_lolbin:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\schtasks.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: parent_iis and parent_exchange_pool and child_lolbin and not filter_machine
falsepositives:
  - Custom Exchange management scripts run via OWA/ECP (rare; baseline before alerting)
  - Exchange CU/SU installer post-actions
level: high
ucs.rationale: Generic 'w3wp spawns cmd' alerts everywhere; what's actor-specific to Silk Typhoon is the combined shape: (a) parent w3wp.exe in an Exchange-app-pool token (MSExchange/OwaAppPool/EcpAppPool/RpcProxyAppPool — the very pools ProxyLogon CVE-2021-26855 hit), (b) child = recon/cmd LOLBin, (c) co-temporal .aspx/.ashx write into the OWA/ECP/HttpProxy auth virtual directories that HAFNIUM dropped China Ch

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon LSASS credential theft on Exchange / IIS hosts (procdump + comsvcs MiniDump)
ucs.description: Hunts Silk Typhoon's documented post-shell credential-access step on freshly-shelled Exchange / OWA / IIS hosts: dropping procdump.exe (often named procdump64.exe or renamed) against lsass.exe, or living-off-the-land via rundll32 comsvcs.dll!MiniDump to harvest the Local Security Authority. The detection is scoped to hosts that have recently run an Exchange or IIS worker process so we catch G0125's web-shell-then-LSASS pivot rather than every red-team procdump.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where ((Processes.process_name IN ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") AND Processes.process="*lsass*") OR (Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" AND Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*" AND Processes.process="*MiniDump*") OR Processes.process="*-accepteula*lsass*-ma*" OR Processes.process="*MiniDumpW*lsass*" OR Processes.process="*Out-Minidump*") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | search [| tstats summariesonly=false count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="w3wp.exe" OR Processes.process_name="MSExchangeHMHost.exe" OR Processes.process_name="MSExchangeHMWorker.exe" OR Processes.process_name="edgetransport.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe") earliest=-30d by host | fields host] | where NOT match(user, "\$$") | table _time host user process_name parent_process_name parent_process process | sort - _time
ucs.kql:
let ExchangeOrIISHosts = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where FileName in~ ("MSExchangeHMHost.exe","MSExchangeHMWorker.exe","edgetransport.exe","Microsoft.Exchange.Directory.TopologyService.exe","w3wp.exe")
       or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | distinct DeviceId;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where DeviceId in (ExchangeOrIISHosts)
| where (FileName has "procdump" and ProcessCommandLine has "lsass")
     or (ProcessCommandLine has_all ("comsvcs.dll","MiniDump"))
     or (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs" and ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump")
     or (ProcessCommandLine has "lsass" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-ma "," -mp ","MiniDumpW","Out-Minidump"))
     or (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "lsass")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ChildBin = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentBin = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let ExchangeOrIISHosts = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName has_any ("\\MSExchangeHMHost.exe","\\MSExchangeHMWorker.exe","\\edgetransport.exe","\\w3wp.exe")
         or ParentProcessName endswith "w3wp.exe"
    | distinct Computer;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where Computer in (ExchangeOrIISHosts)
| where (NewProcessName has "procdump" and CommandLine has "lsass")
     or (CommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and CommandLine has "MiniDump")
     or (NewProcessName endswith "\\rundll32.exe" and CommandLine has "comsvcs" and CommandLine has "MiniDump")
     or (CommandLine has "lsass" and (CommandLine has "-ma " or CommandLine has " -mp " or CommandLine has "MiniDumpW" or CommandLine has "Out-Minidump"))
     or (ParentProcessName endswith "w3wp.exe" and CommandLine has "lsass")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Silk Typhoon's 2021 ProxyLogon playbook (Volexity/MSTIC) and the 2024-2025 IT-supply-chain campaigns repeatedly chain web-shell access on Exchange/IIS into procdump.exe -ma against lsass.exe (T1003.001 is in their listed technique set) and the comsvcs.dll!MiniDump rundll32 LOLBin. The actor-specific narrowing here is host-scope: we restrict to devices that recently hosted an Exchange/IIS worker, w

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?
